description: Gateway Template is applied to a site for gateway(s) in a site.

additional_config_cmds.description: additional CLI commands to append to the generated Junos config. **Note**: no check is done

items.description: JUNOS "set" command to add to the generated configuration

additionalProperties.description: BFD is enabled when either bfd_minimum_interval or bfd_multiplier is configured

bfd_minimum_interval.description:
Optional if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`, when bfd_multiplier is configured alone. Default:
  * 1000 if `type`==`external`
  * 350 `type`==`internal`

bfd_multiplier.description: Optional if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`, when bfd_minimum_interval_is_configured alone

disable_bfd.description: Optional if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`. BFD provides faster path failure detection and is enabled by default

export_policy.description: Default export policies if no per-neighbor policies defined

extended_v4_nexthop.description: Optional if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`. By default, either inet/net6 unicast depending on neighbor IP family (v4 or v6). For v6 neighbors, to exchange v4 nexthop, which allows dual-stack support, enable this

import_policy.description: Optional if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`. Default import policies if no per-neighbor policies defined

local_as.description: Required if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`. BGP AS, value in range 1-4294967295

disabled.description: If true, the BGP session to this neighbor will be administratively disabled/shutdown

multihop_ttl.description: Assuming BGP neighbor is directly connected

neighbors.description: Required if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`. If per-neighbor as is desired. Property key is the neighbor address

networks.description: Optional if `via`==`lan`. List of networks where we expect BGP neighbor to connect to/from

no_private_as.description: Optional if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`. If true, we will not advertise private ASNs (AS 64512-65534) to this neighbor

no_readvertise_to_overlay.description: Optional if `via`==`lan`, `via`==`tunnel` or `via`==`wan`. By default, we'll re-advertise all learned BGP routers toward overlay

created_time.description: When the object has been created, in epoch

dns_servers.description: If `type`==`local` or `type6`==`local` - optional, if not defined, system one will be used

dns_suffix.description: If `type`==`local` or `type6`==`local` - optional, if not defined, system one will be used

fixed_bindings.description: If `type`==`local` or `type6`==`local`. Property key is the MAC Address. Format is `[0-9a-f]{12}` (e.g. "5684dae9ac8b")

gateway.description: If `type`==`local` - optional, `ip` will be used if not provided

lease_time.description: In seconds, lease time has to be between 3600 [1hr] - 604800 [1 week], default is 86400 [1 day]

options.description: If `type`==`local` or `type6`==`local`. Property key is the DHCP option number

server_id_override.description:
`server_id_override`==`true` means the device, when acts as DHCP relay and forwards DHCP responses from DHCP server to clients, 
should overwrite the Sever Identifier option (i.e. DHCP option 54) in DHCP responses with its own IP address.

vendor_encapsulated.description:
If `type`==`local` or `type6`==`local`. Property key is <enterprise number>:<sub option code>, with
  * enterprise number: 1-65535 (https://www.iana.org/assignments/enterprise-numbers/enterprise-numbers)
  * sub option code: 1-255, sub-option code

enabled.description: If set to `false`, disable the DHCP server

dns_servers.description: Global dns settings. To keep compatibility, dns settings in `ip_config` and `oob_ip_config` will overwrite this setting

dns_suffix.description: Global dns settings. To keep compatibility, dns settings in `ip_config` and `oob_ip_config` will overwrite this setting

extra_routes.description: Property key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "10.0.0.0/8"), the destination Network name or a variable (e.g. "{{myvar}}")

extra_routes6.description: Property key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "2a02:1234:420a:10c9::/64"), the destination Network name or a variable (e.g. "{{myvar}}")

additionalProperties.description: property key defines the type of matching. e.g: `match_name[0:3]`, `match_model[0-6]` or `match_role`

additional_config_cmds.description: additional CLI commands to append to the generated Junos config. **Note**: no check is done

items.description: JUNOS "set" command to add to the generated configuration

ae_disable_lacp.description: To disable LACP support for the AE interface

ae_idx.description: Users could force to use the designated AE name

disable_autoneg.description: If `speed` and `duplex` are specified, whether to disable autonegotiation

dynamic_usage.description: Enable dynamic usage for this port. Set to `dynamic` to enable.

mtu.description: Media maximum transmission unit (MTU) is the largest data unit that can be forwarded without fragmentation

no_local_overwrite.description: Prevent helpdesk to override the port config

port_network.description: Required if `usage`==`vlan_tunnel`. Q-in-Q tunneling using All-in-one bundling. This also enables standard L2PT for interfaces that are not encapsulation tunnel interfaces and uses MAC rewrite operation. [View more information](https://www.juniper.net/documentation/us/en/software/junos/multicast-l2/topics/topic-map/q-in-q.html#id-understanding-qinq-tunneling-and-vlan-translation)

usage.description: Port usage name. For Q-in-Q, use `vlan_tunnel`. If EVPN is used, use `evpn_uplink`or `evpn_downlink`

port_config.description: Property key is the port name or range (e.g. "ge-0/0/0-10")

id.description: Unique ID of the object instance in the Mist Organization

created_time.description: When the object has been created, in epoch

id.description: Unique ID of the object instance in the Mist Organization

modified_time.description: When the object has been modified for the last time, in epoch

action.description:
enum:
  * alert (default)
  * drop: silently dropping packets
  * close: notify client/server to close connection

idp_profiles.description: Property key is the profile name

secondary_ips.description: Optional list of secondary IPs in CIDR format

ip_configs.description: Property key is the network name

modified_time.description: When the object has been modified for the last time, in epoch

items.description: Networks are usually subnets that have cross-site significance. `networks`in Org Settings will got merged into `networks`in Site Setting. For gateways, they can be used to define Service Routes.

created_time.description: When the object has been created, in epoch

disallow_mist_services.description: Whether to disallow Mist Devices in the network

id.description: Unique ID of the object instance in the Mist Organization

internet_access.description: Whether this network has direct internet access

internal_ip.description: The Destination NAT destination IP Address. Must be an IP (i.e. "192.168.70.30") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

port.description: The Destination NAT destination IP Address. Must be a Port (i.e. "443") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

wan_name.description: SRX Only. If not set, we configure the nat policies against all WAN ports for simplicity

destination_nat.description: Property key can be an External IP (i.e. "63.16.0.3"), an External IP:Port (i.e. "63.16.0.3:443"), an External Port (i.e. ":443"), an External CIDR (i.e. "63.16.0.0/30"), an External CIDR:Port (i.e. "63.16.0.0/30:443") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}"). At least one of the `internal_ip` or `port` must be defined

restricted.description: By default, all access is allowed, to only allow certain traffic, make `restricted`=`true` and define service_policies

internal_ip.description: The Static NAT destination IP Address. Must be an IP Address (i.e. "192.168.70.3") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

wan_name.description: SRX Only. If not set, we configure the nat policies against all WAN ports for simplicity. Can be a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

static_nat.description: Property key may be an External IP Address (i.e. "63.16.0.3"), a CIDR (i.e. "63.16.0.12/20") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

isolation.description: Whether to allow clients in the network to talk to each other

modified_time.description: When the object has been modified for the last time, in epoch

multicast.description: Whether to enable multicast support (only PIM-sparse mode is supported)

disable_igmp.description: If the network will only be the source of the multicast traffic, IGMP can be disabled

groups.description: Group address to RP (rendezvous point) mapping. Property Key is the CIDR (example "225.1.0.3/32")

routed_for_networks.description: For a Network (usually LAN), it can be routable to other networks (e.g. OSPF)

items.description: The user/tenant IP Address (i.e. "192.168.70.30"), an Subnet (i.e. "192.168.70.0/24") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

tenants.description: Property key must be the user/tenant name (i.e. "printer-1") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

advertised_subnet.description: If `routed`==`true`, whether to advertise an aggregated subnet toward HUB this is useful when there are multiple networks on SPOKE's side

allow_ping.description: Whether to allow ping from vpn into this routed network

internal_ip.description: The Destination NAT destination IP Address. Must be an IP (i.e. "192.168.70.30") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

destination_nat.description: Property key can be an External IP (i.e. "63.16.0.3"), an External IP:Port (i.e. "63.16.0.3:443"), an External Port (i.e. ":443"), an External CIDR (i.e. "63.16.0.0/30"), an External CIDR:Port (i.e. "63.16.0.0/30:443") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}"). At least one of the `internal_ip` or `port` must be defined

nat_pool.description: If `routed`==`false` (usually at Spoke), but some hosts needs to be reachable from Hub, a subnet is required to create and advertise the route to Hub

no_readvertise_to_overlay.description: toward overlay, how HUB should deal with routes it received from Spokes

other_vrfs.description: By default, the routes are only readvertised toward the same vrf on spoke. To allow it to be leaked to other vrfs

source_nat.description: If `routed`==`false` (usually at Spoke), but some hosts needs to be reachable from Hub

internal_ip.description: The Static NAT destination IP Address. Must be an IP Address (i.e. "192.168.70.3") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

static_nat.description: Property key may be an External IP Address (i.e. "63.16.0.3"), a CIDR (i.e. "63.16.0.12/20") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

summarized_subnet.description: toward overlay, how HUB should deal with routes it received from Spokes

vpn_access.description: Property key is the VPN name. Whether this network can be accessed from vpn

ntp_servers.description: List of NTP servers specific to this device. By default, those in Site Settings will be used

node1.description: For HA Cluster, node1 can have different IP Config

netmask.description: Used only if `subnet` is not specified in `networks`

use_mgmt_vrf.description: If supported on the platform. If enabled, DNS will be using this routing-instance, too

use_mgmt_vrf_for_host_out.description: Whether to use `mgmt_junos` for host-out traffic (NTP/TACPLUS/RADIUS/SYSLOG/SNMP), if alternative source network/ip is desired

use_mgmt_vrf.description: If supported on the platform. If enabled, DNS will be using this routing-instance, too

use_mgmt_vrf_for_host_out.description: For host-out traffic (NTP/TACPLUS/RADIUS/SYSLOG/SNMP), if alternative source network/ip is desired

disabled.description: For SSR Only. `true`, if this specific path is undesired

gateway_ip.description: Only if `type`==`local`, if a different gateway is desired

internet_access.description: Only if `type`==`vpn`, if this vpn path can be used for internet

name.description:
Required when 
  * `type`==`vpn`: the name of the VPN Path to use 
  * `type`==`wan`: the name of the WAN interface to use

target_ips.description: If `type`==`local`, if destination IP is to be replaced

path_preferences.description: Property key is the path name

ae_disable_lacp.description: If `aggregated`==`true`. To disable LCP support for the AE interface

ae_idx.description: If `aggregated`==`true`. Users could force to use the designated AE name (must be an integer between 0 and 127)

ae_lacp_force_up.description: For SRX Only, if `aggregated`==`true`.Sets the state of the interface as UP when the peer has limited LACP capability. Use case: When a device connected to this AE port is ZTPing for the first time, it will not have LACP configured on the other end. **Note:** Turning this on will enable force-up on one of the interfaces in the bundle only

critical.description: To generate port up/down alarm, set it to true

description.description: Interface Description. Can be a variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

gateway.description: Except for out-of_band interface (vme/em0/fxp0). Interface Default Gateway IP Address (i.e. "192.168.1.1") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

gateway6.description: Except for out-of_band interface (vme/em0/fxp0). Interface Default Gateway IPv6 Address (i.e. "2001:db8::1") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

ip.description: Interface IP Address (i.e. "192.168.1.8") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

ip6.description: Interface IPv6 Address (i.e. "2001:db8::123") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

netmask.description: Used only if `subnet` is not specified in `networks`. Interface Netmask (i.e. "/24") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

netmask6.description: Used only if `subnet` is not specified in `networks`. Interface IPv6 Netmask (i.e. "/64") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

network.description: Optional, the network to be used for mgmt

name.description: Name that we'll use to derive config

networks.description: If `usage`==`lan`, name of the [networks]($h/Orgs%20Networks/_overview) to attach to the interface

port_network.description: Only for SRX and if `usage`==`lan`, the name of the Network to be used as the Untagged VLAN

preserve_dscp.description: Whether to preserve dscp when sending traffic over VPN (SSR-only)

redundant_group.description: If HA mode, SRX Only - support redundancy-group. 1-128 for physical SRX, 1-64 for virtual SRX

reth_idx.description: For SRX only and if HA Mode

reth_nodes.description: SSR only - supporting vlan-based redundancy (matching the size of `networks`)

ssr_no_virtual_mac.description: When SSR is running as VM, this is required on certain hosting platforms

class_percentages.description: percentages for different class of traffic: high / medium / low / best-effort. Sum must be equal to 100

vlan_id.description: If WAN interface is on a VLAN. Can be the VLAN ID (i.e. "10") or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}")

bfd_use_tunnel_mode.description: Only if the VPN `type`==`hub_spoke`. Whether to use tunnel mode. SSR only

preference.description: Only if the VPN `type`==`hub_spoke`. For a given VPN, when `path_selection.strategy`==`simple`, the preference for a path (lower is preferred)

role.description: If the VPN `type`==`hub_spoke`, enum: `hub`, `spoke`. If the VPN `type`==`mesh`, enum: `mesh`

class_percentages.description: percentages for different class of traffic: high / medium / low / best-effort. Sum must be equal to 100

vpn_paths.description: Property key is the VPN name

wan_ext_ip.description: Only if `usage`==`wan`, optional. If spoke should reach this port by a different IP

wan_ext_ip6.description: Only if `usage`==`wan`, optional. If spoke should reach this port by a different IPv6

wan_extra_routes.description: Only if `usage`==`wan`. Property Key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "100.100.100.0/24")

wan_extra_routes6.description: Only if `usage`==`wan`. Property Key is the destination CIDR (e.g. "2a02:1234:420a:10c9::/64")

wan_networks.description: Only if `usage`==`wan`. If some networks are connected to this WAN port, it can be added here so policies can be defined

wan_source_nat.description: Only if `usage`==`wan`, optional. By default, source-NAT is performed on all WAN Ports using the interface-ip

port_config.description: Property key is the Port Name (i.e. "ge-0/0/0"), the Ports Range (i.e. "ge-0/0/0-10"), the List of Ports (i.e. "ge-0/0/0,ge-1/0/0", only allowed for Aggregated or Redundant interfaces) or a Variable (i.e. "{{myvar}}").

terms.description: zero or more criteria/filter can be specified to match the term, all criteria have to be met

add_target_vrfs.description: For SSR, hub decides how VRF routes are leaked on spoke

community.description: When used as export policy, optional

exclude_as_path.description: When used as export policy, optional. To exclude certain AS

export_communities.description: When used as export policy, optional

local_preference.description: Optional, for an import policy, local_preference can be changed

prepend_as_path.description: When used as export policy, optional. By default, the local AS will be prepended, to change it

matching.description: zero or more criteria/filter can be specified to match the term, all criteria have to be met

prefix.description: zero or more criteria/filter can be specified to match the term, all criteria have to be met

vrf_name.description: Name of the vrf instance, it can also be the name of the VPN or wan if they

vpn_neighbor_mac.description: overlay-facing criteria (used for bgp_config where via=vpn)

vpn_path.description: overlay-facing criteria (used for bgp_config where via=vpn). ordered-

routing_policies.description: Property key is the routing policy name

avprofile_id.description: org-level AV Profile can be used, this takes precedence over 'profile'

profile.description: Default / noftp / httponly / or keys from av_profiles

block_message.example: Access to this URL Category has been blocked

idpprofile_id.description: org_level IDP Profile can be used, this takes precedence over `profile`

path_preference.description: By default, we derive all paths available and use them. Optionally, you can customize by using `path_preference`

secintelprofile_id.description: org-level secintel Profile can be used, this takes precedence over 'profile'

servicepolicy_id.description: Used to link servicepolicy defined at org level and overwrite some attributes

auto_provision.description: Auto Provisioning configuration for the tunne. This takes precedence over the `primary` and `secondary` nodes.

enabled.description: Enable auto provisioning for the tunnel. If enabled, the `primary` and `secondary` nodes will be ignored.

region.description: API override for POP selection in the case user wants to override the auto discovery of remote network location and force the tunnel to use the specified peer location.

service_connection.description: if `provider`==`prisma-ipsec`. By default, we'll use the location of the site to determine the optimal Remote Network location, optionally, service_connection can be considered, then we'll also consider this along with the site location. Define service_connection if the traffic is to be routed to a specific service connection. This field takes a service connection name that is configured in the Prisma cloud, Prisma Access Setup -> Service Connections.

dh_group.description:
Only if `provider`==`custom-ipsec`. enum:
  * 1
  * 2 (1024-bit)
  * 5
  * 14 (default, 2048-bit)
  * 15 (3072-bit)
  * 16 (4096-bit)
  * 19 (256-bit ECP)
  * 20 (384-bit ECP)
  * 21 (521-bit ECP)
  * 24 (2048-bit ECP)

local_subnets.description: List of Local protected subnet for policy-based IPSec negotiation

networks.description: If `provider`==`custom-ipsec` or `provider`==`prisma-ipsec`, networks reachable via this tunnel

items.description: IP Address of the remote host

interval.description: How often to trigger the probe

threshold.description: Number of consecutive misses before declaring the tunnel down

timeout.description: Time within which to complete the connectivity check

remote_subnets.description: List of Remote protected subnet for policy-based IPSec negotiation

items.description: IP Address of the remote host

tunnel_configs.description: Property key is the tunnel name

jse.description: For jse-ipsec, this allows provisioning of adequate resource on JSE. Make sure adequate licenses are added

org_name.description: JSE Organization name. The list of available organizations can be retrieved with the [Get Org JSE Info](/#operations/getOrgJseInfo) API Call

service_account_name.description: For prisma-ipsec, service account name to used for tunnel auto provisioning

aup_enabled.description: Can only be `true` when `auth_required`==`false`, display Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)

aup_force_ssl_inspection.description: Proxy HTTPs traffic, requiring Zscaler cert to be installed in browser

aup_timeout_in_days.description: Required if `aup_enabled`==`true`. Days before AUP is requested again

auth_required.description: Enable this option to enforce user authentication

caution_enabled.description: Can only be `true` when `auth_required`==`false`, display caution notification for non-authenticated users

dn_bandwidth.description: Download bandwidth cap of the link, in Mbps. Disabled if not set

idle_time_in_minutes.description: Required if `surrogate_IP`==`true`, idle Time to Disassociation

ofw_enabled.description: If `true`, enable the firewall control option

sub_locations.description: `sub-locations` can be used for specific uses cases to define different configuration based on the user network

aup_enabled.description: Can only be `true` when `auth_required`==`false`, display Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)

aup_force_ssl_inspection.description: Proxy HTTPs traffic, requiring Zscaler cert to be installed in browser

aup_timeout_in_days.description: Required if `aup_enabled`==`true`. Days before AUP is requested again

auth_required.description: Enable this option to authenticate users

caution_enabled.description: Can only be `true` when `auth_required`==`false`, display caution notification for non-authenticated users

dn_bandwidth.description: Download bandwidth cap of the link, in Mbps. Disabled if not set

idle_time_in_minutes.description: Required if `surrogate_IP`==`true`, idle Time to Disassociation

ofw_enabled.description: If `true`, enable the firewall control option

surrogate_IP.description: Can only be `true` when `auth_required`==`true`. Map a user to a private IP address so it applies the user's policies, instead of the location's policies

surrogate_IP_enforced_for_known_browsers.description: Can only be `true` when `surrogate_IP`==`true`, enforce surrogate IP for known browsers

surrogate_refresh_time_in_minutes.description: Required if `surrogate_IP_enforced_for_known_browsers`==`true`, must be lower or equal than `idle_time_in_minutes`, refresh Time for re-validation of Surrogacy

up_bandwidth.description: Download bandwidth cap of the link, in Mbps. Disabled if not set

surrogate_IP.description: Can only be `true` when `auth_required`==`true`. Map a user to a private IP address so it applies the user's policies, instead of the location's policies

surrogate_IP_enforced_for_known_browsers.description: Can only be `true` when `surrogate_IP`==`true`, enforce surrogate IP for known browsers

surrogate_refresh_time_in_minutes.description: Required if `surrogate_IP_enforced_for_known_browsers`==`true`, must be lower or equal than `idle_time_in_minutes`, refresh Time for re-validation of Surrogacy

up_bandwidth.description: Download bandwidth cap of the link, in Mbps. Disabled if not set

xff_forward_enabled.description: Location uses proxy chaining to forward traffic

url_filtering_deny_msg.default: Access to this URL Category has been blocked
url_filtering_deny_msg.description: When a service policy denies a app_category, what message to show in user's browser
url_filtering_deny_msg.example: Access to this URL Category has been blocked

enabled.description: Whether to enable VRF (when supported on the device)

vrf_instances.description: Property key is the network name
